summary: Estimated fetal weight from ultrasound serves as an indicator rather than a direct modifiable variable; however, underlying conditions like poor glucose control that influence its accuracy are treatable and thus represent actionable risks affecting cesarean rates. Effective maternal condition management can reduce abnormal Fetal Weight estimates leading to fewer unnecessary C-sections.
